---
title: "Project 1"
author: "Radzhana Rabdanova and Chingiz Rabdanov"
format: html
editor: visual
output: html_document
---


# --- Project 1: Chess Tournament Data Transformation ---
# Author: Radzhana Rabdanova and Chingiz Randanov

library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(DT) # For interactive tables

# 1. Loading
# Update the path to your local file location
path <- "C:/Users/radzh/OneDrive/Desktop/data 607/project1/tournamentinfo.txt"
raw_data <- readLines(path)

# 2. Cleaning: removing lines that contain no letters or numbers (including dashes)
# This ensures we only work with actual text data
clean_data <- raw_data[str_detect(raw_data, "[A-Za-z0-9]")]

# 3. Finding where the first player starts
# In chess tables, this is usually a line starting with "1"
start_index <- grep("^\\s*1\\s*\\|", clean_data)[1] 
if(is.na(start_index)) start_index <- 3

# If not found, skip headers
clean_data <- clean_data[start_index:length(clean_data)]

player_list <- list()

# 4. Parsing loop
for (i in seq(1, length(clean_data), by = 2)) { 
  if ((i + 1) > length(clean_data)) break

  line1 <- str_split(clean_data[i], "\\|")[[1]] 
  line2 <- str_split(clean_data[i+1], "\\|")[[1]]

  # Check for minimum number of columns
  if (length(line1) < 3) next

  id <- as.numeric(str_trim(line1[1])) 
  if (is.na(id)) next

  # Extracting fields according to the assignment
  name <- str_trim(line1[2]) 
  state <- str_trim(line2[1]) 
  total_pts <- as.numeric(str_trim(line1[3]))

  # Finding Pre-Rating (numbers after 'R:')
  pre_rating <- as.numeric(str_extract(line2[2], "(?<=R: )\\s*\\d{3,4}"))

  # Finding Opponent IDs using a refined regex to capture IDs after W, L, or D
  opp_id_raw <- str_extract_all(clean_data[i], "\\|\\s*[WLD]\\s+(\\d+)")
  opponents <- as.numeric(str_extract(opp_id_raw[[1]], "\\d+"))

  player_list[[id]] <- list( 
    Name = name, 
    State = state, 
    TotalPoints = total_pts, 
    PreRating = pre_rating, 
    Opponents = opponents 
  ) 
}

# 5. Building the table and calculating the average
final_results <- map_df(player_list, function(p) { 
  if (is.null(p)) return(NULL)

  # Getting opponent ratings
  opp_ratings <- sapply(p$Opponents, function(oid) {      
    if (!is.na(oid) && oid <= length(player_list) && !is.null(player_list[[oid]])) {       
      player_list[[oid]]$PreRating 
    } else { 
      NA 
    } 
  })

  avg_rating <- round(mean(opp_ratings, na.rm = TRUE), 0)

  data.frame( 
    Player_Name = p$Name,      
    State = p$State, 
    Total_Points = p$TotalPoints,      
    Pre_Rating = p$PreRating, 
    Avg_Opponent_Rating = avg_rating, 
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE 
  ) 
})

# 6. Interactive Visualization
# Using DT library to create a searchable table for the final report
datatable(final_results, 
          caption = "Chess Tournament Results: Player Performance Overview",
          options = list(pageLength = 10, searchHighlight = TRUE),
          filter = 'top')

# 7. Histogram
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(final_results, aes(x = Avg_Opponent_Rating)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 50, fill = "skyblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(aes(xintercept = mean(Avg_Opponent_Rating, na.rm = TRUE)), 
             color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Average Opponent Ratings",
       subtitle = "The red line indicates the tournament mean",
       x = "Average Opponent Rating",
       y = "Number of Players") +
  theme_minimal()
